articles.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
articles.summary: Granola AI note-taking app shares notes with anyone holding a link by default and uses them for internal AI training unless users opt out, raising privacy concerns despite claiming private defaults.

articles.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
articles.summary: Elon Musk may file for SpaceX's IPO in early April, with the earliest public offering expected in June pending SEC review. The exact timing remains uncertain but June 7th is a potential target date.

articles.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a popular weekday livestream talk show featuring interviews with AI executives and tech leaders. The show broadcasts on X and YouTube, competing with major financial news networks.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a popular Silicon Valley tech podcast. The show will maintain independence while being overseen by OpenAI's chief political operative Chris Lehane.

articles.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
articles.summary: Microsoft released three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text and generating audio and images, six months after forming the group.

articles.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
articles.summary: Google's Vids app now allows users to customize and direct avatars through text prompts for video creation, enhancing the app's video production capabilities.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing developer frustration with legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks developer backlash, while Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar AI coding capabilities without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco. The startup used an AI token puzzle decoded into a coding challenge to attract talented engineers.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered new Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs that allow malicious users to gain complete root control of shared cloud machines. The attacks exploit memory bit flips caused by rapid DRAM access.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, potentially threatening blockchain security within minutes using neutral atom qubits.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google moved its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current encryption protecting military, financial, and government secrets. The company urges widespread adoption of post-quantum cryptography algorithms.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from global conflicts are expected to increase plastic costs, as plastics derive from petrochemicals affected by supply chain disruptions. American consumers will likely experience the economic ripple effects.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions are cascading into plastic shortages, as petrochemicals face supply chain disruptions. Plastics pervade modern life from clothing to electronics, making price increases economically significant.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers worldwide use smartphones to record themselves performing daily tasks, providing video data to train humanoid robots. Companies like Micro1 employ thousands across 50+ countries to collect this training data.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, an advanced multimodal AI model designed for on-device processing. It combines frontier-level intelligence with the ability to run locally on consumer hardware.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 breaks new ground in computer use AI, advancing artificial intelligence capabilities for human-computer interaction and automation tasks.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI vision capabilities with improved object detection and scene understanding. The technology enhances machine perception for autonomous systems and computer vision applications.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development and upcoming trends in the field. The conversation explores what innovations and challenges lie ahead for artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline workflows and produce more imaginative marketing materials.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for advancing farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google launches two new Gemini API inference tiers: Flex for cost-effective solutions and Priority for low-latency performance, allowing developers to balance expenses and reliability.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing features using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model enhances audio AI with improved precision and reduced latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI's potential for harmful manipulation in finance and healthcare, developing new safety measures to protect people.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: Kilo launched KiloClaw to address shadow AI deployments where employees bypass official procurement to deploy autonomous agents on personal infrastructure. The platform provides governance and visibility over decentralized agent deployments to protect enterprise data.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered defense strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include enforcing strict access governance and encryption to limit exposure to sensitive models.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan through 2030 prioritizes AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology, emphasizing development of high-performance chips, software, and advanced model architectures. Communications infrastructure including 5G+ and 6G networks will support AI deployment.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging capabilities for collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in diverse indoor environments by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, taking advantage of the OpenClaw AI agent boom.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a historical limitation in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering techniques enable LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, using parabolic extrapolation to develop testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Arcee AI Releases Trinity Large Thinking: An Apache 2.0 Open Reasoning Model for Long-Horizon Agents and Tool Use
articles.summary: Arcee AI released Trinity Large Thinking, an open-source reasoning model under Apache 2.0 license designed for complex multi-step reasoning, autonomous agents, and tool use.

articles.title: Defeating the ‘Token Tax’: How Google Gemma 4, NVIDIA, and OpenClaw are Revolutionizing Local Agentic AI: From RTX Desktops to DGX Spark
articles.summary: Google Gemma 4 and NVIDIA hardware enable local AI agents without cloud token costs. Developers can now build personalized, always-on AI assistants on edge devices using OpenClaw.

articles.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 3B Vision: A New Vision Language Model for Enterprise Grade Document Data Extraction
articles.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a specialized vision-language model for enterprise document data extraction. The modular approach prioritizes structured data accuracy over general image captioning.

articles.title: Brick vs. Bloom Card: I tested both for my screen addiction, and the winner depends on you
articles.summary: Brick and Bloom Card are apps that block distracting apps to combat screen addiction. Brick offers a cheaper alternative with unique features, though both have tradeoffs.

articles.title: I turned to PrivacyBee to clean up my data - here's how it made me disappear
articles.summary: PrivacyBee is a comprehensive data removal service that eliminates personal information across hundreds of websites, helping users disappear from the internet.

articles.title: How to switch from ChatGPT to Gemini - without starting from scratch
articles.summary: Google Gemini now allows users to transfer memories, chat history, and preferences from other AI chatbots like ChatGPT, eliminating the need to restart conversations and settings.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards to more visible applications. Key trends emerging in 2026 show ML becoming more integrated into user-facing products.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where execution pipelines are intentionally halted to implement human-in-the-loop approval gates for autonomous agents.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: This article explains LLM inference stages: prefill phase with attention mechanisms, decode phase for token generation, and KV cache optimization techniques to improve decoding efficiency.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: This article explains linear regression through vector projections, connecting geometric projection concepts to predictive modeling. It demonstrates how least squares can be understood as a projection problem.

articles.title: How to Handle Classical Data in Quantum Models
articles.summary: Article explores workflows and encoding techniques for integrating classical data into quantum machine learning models, addressing a key challenge in hybrid quantum-classical computing.

articles.title: Quantum Simulations with Python
articles.summary: Learn to run quantum experiments using Qiskit-Aer, a Python library for quantum simulations. This guide enables developers to perform quantum computing tasks programmatically.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local, on-device AI execution across RTX GPUs and personal AI supercomputers. These small, fast models enable efficient agentic AI with real-time context access beyond cloud computing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, expanding its cloud gaming library with fresh titles.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that operate as intelligent grid assets, enabling faster grid connections, improved efficiency, and enhanced grid reliability through real-time energy orchestration.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals framework enables systematic evaluation of AI agents through multiple evaluators assessing helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage. The platform extends beyond single-turn interactions to support multi-turn agent evaluation with realistic user simulation.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation model training using SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding context windows. This reduced training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables AI agents to browse the web safely by using AWS Network Firewall to control domain access and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration while filtering malicious sites.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 accelerates vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match improved model throughput. The approach addresses bottlenecks in data processing to enable high-performance vision AI systems.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches with multimodal and multilingual capabilities, scaling from data centers to edge devices like NVIDIA Jetson for flexible deployment options.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference achieves single-digit microsecond latency for algorithmic trading, enabling faster response times in high-speed electronic markets using advanced neural network models.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations view social media ads across platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward advertising on social media.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technologies shared openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A professional with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in the XR field.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and AI safety. The work uses feature attribution techniques to make LLM decision-making more transparent.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: This research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimizes how well measurements distinguish objects using noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm addresses TD learning's scalability issues, enabling better performance on long-horizon tasks without temporal difference learning.
